personas.name: Workflow Author
personas.role: Writing durable workflows in Gleam using the aion_flow SDK

stories.text: As a workflow author, I want my activity calls to be durable so that if the engine crashes and restarts, the activity result is returned from history instead of re-dispatching to the worker.
As a workflow author, I want workflow.now to return a deterministic timestamp so that my workflow logic produces the same decisions on replay as on first execution.
As a workflow author, I want workflow.random to return a deterministic value so that randomised branching in my workflow replays correctly.
As a workflow author, I want to sleep for a duration and have the engine wake my workflow after that time so that I can implement polling, backoff, and scheduled delays.
As a workflow author, I want to receive signals from external callers so that my workflow can react to events like approvals, cancellations, or data updates.
As a workflow author, I want to register query handlers so that external callers can inspect my workflow's current state without modifying it.
As a workflow author, I want to spawn child workflows and await their results so that I can compose complex workflows from simpler building blocks.
As a workflow author, I want to dispatch multiple activities in parallel and collect all results so that I can fan out work for better throughput.
As a workflow author, I want to race multiple activities and take the first result so that I can implement timeout patterns and competitive dispatch.

personas.name: Platform Operator
personas.role: Running and monitoring Aion workflows in production

stories.text: As a platform operator, I want completed workflows to show a full event history so that I can audit what happened, when, and in what order.
As a platform operator, I want workflow completion to be detected automatically so that workflows do not stay in Running status after their process exits.
As a platform operator, I want crashed workflows to be recoverable by restarting the engine so that I have Temporal-class durability guarantees.

personas.name: Temporal Developer
personas.role: Evaluating Aion as a Temporal replacement

stories.text: As a Temporal developer, I want the getting-started tutorial to demonstrate a complete workflow lifecycle (start, activity, complete, describe history) so that I can evaluate whether Aion meets my durability expectations.
As a Temporal developer, I want activities, timers, signals, queries, and child workflows to work as I expect from Temporal so that I can port my existing workflow patterns without surprises.
